fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: New Mexico Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to New Mexico - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity new mexico, how to transfer physical therapist license to new mexico, physical therapist compact new mexico, new mexico physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: New Mexico Physical Therapy Board
board.parent_dept: New Mexico Regulation and Licensing Department
board.mailing_address: New Mexico Physical Therapy Board, P.O. Box 25101, Santa Fe, NM 87504

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in New Mexico.

steps.title: Create Account and Start Application on CONSIVA
steps.description: Navigate to the CONSIVA portal, register for a new account by providing required personal information for verification, and then log in to select the 'Physical Therapist by Examination' or 'Physical Therapist by Endorsement' application.

steps.title: Submit Application and Pay Fee
steps.description: Complete the online application form within CONSIVA, upload all necessary documentation as prompted (e.g., official transcripts, NPTE scores if applicable), and pay the non-refundable application fee through the portal.

steps.title: Arrange for Official Transcripts
steps.description: Have your official academic transcripts, verifying your DPT or equivalent degree, sent directly from your educational institution to the New Mexico Physical Therapy Board.

steps.title: Request NPTE Score Transfer (for Exam applicants)
steps.description: If applying by examination or endorsement, arrange for your National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE) scores to be sent directly to the New Mexico Physical Therapy Board by the Federation of State Boards of Physical Therapy (FSBPT).

steps.title: Complete Background Check
steps.description: Follow instructions provided within your CONSIVA application or by the Board regarding fingerprinting and a criminal background check, if required for your application type.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly log into your CONSIVA account to check the status of your application, respond promptly to any requests for additional information, and await notification of your license approval.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a New Mexico physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does New Mexico have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. New Mexico participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in New Mexico without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to New Mexico?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in New Mexico while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in New Mexico
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in New Mexico, applicants must use the New Mexico Regulation and Licensing Department's online licensing portal, CONSIVA.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the CONSIVA portal, click 'Register,' agree to terms, provide personal verification information (SSN, DOB, etc.), create a username and password, then log in to select the appropriate application type.
